free_text: Neutron activation cross section measurement from
Li-7(p,n) in the proton energy region of 20 MeV to
Data for C, Na, Mg, Mn, Fe, Cu, Zn, Au given
Data for Co and (final) Au given
The neutron spectrum was measured at
the TOF facility of the Cyclotron and Radioisotope
performed at the SF cyclotron of the Institute for
Nuclear Study, INS, at the University of Tokyo.
using a 22 mm diameter by 2 mm thick Li target,
enriched to 99.98% in Li-7, on a 12 mm thick graphite
backing, which stopped the beam. The true activation
rates were obtained as the difference between runs
with the Li on graphite target, and runs with a pure
graphite target of the same thickness.
The neutron peak consisted of transitions to the ground
state and first excited state at 0.43 MeV in Be-7. The
spectrum was determined as the difference between runs
with the Li on graphite target and the pure graphite
target, using time-of-flight techniques. The spectrum
was measured in the angular region 0 - 125 degrees.
Natural samples of C, Al, Na, Mn and Au were used.
Enriched samples were also used, with enrichments as
The samples were placed at 10 cm from the Li target.
A 127 mm diameter by 127 mm thick NE213 liquid
scintillation detector was used to measure the neutron
spectrum. TOF techniques were employed, using a flight
A high purity germanium detector was used to
measure the gamma-ray activities of the samples. The
detector peak efficiency was calibrated with several
The peak counts of identified gamma-rays were analysed
The neutron intensity was accurately determined by
measuring the Be-7 yield in the target, using gamma-
Data were corrected for the sum-coincidence effect.
Data were also corrected for activation by low-energy
neutrons, i.e. lower than 15 to 20 MeV, by using data
Co and Au data from NIM A389(1997)463
